title: AI Prices Soar: Fable's Fall & China's Rise? #shorts
summary: Frontier AI models face rising costs and vulnerabilities. Geopolitical competition is driving the emergence of cheaper Chinese open-source AI alternatives, which could reshape the global AI landscape.
writeup: This short from Cyril Imhof argues that frontier AI models are getting more expensive and exposing new vulnerabilities. It frames geopolitical competition as the force behind cheaper Chinese open-source alternatives. The takeaway: rising costs at the frontier and the rise of low-cost open-source models out of China could reshape the global AI landscape, with implications spanning enterprise AI and geopolitics.
